title: Tromsø Winter Adventure.

trains.name: Trains & Taxi Transfers
trains.blurb: A fully escorted winter base in Tromsø with private airport transfers and a full programme of Arctic excursions.

transport.1: Private transfer from Tromsø Airport to your hotel on arrival.
transport.2: Tromsø Island Tour with Arctic Cathedral, including transport across the sound.
transport.3: Full-day snowmobile safari into the Arctic wilderness surrounding Tromsø, approx. 7 hours.
transport.4: Winter whale watching boat tour into the fjords around Tromsø, approx. 7 hours.
transport.5: Private transfer from your hotel to Tromsø Airport for departure.

inclusions: Private transfer Tromsø Airport to hotel on arrival
Tromsø Island Tour with Arctic Cathedral
Night Reindeer Sledding Tour with Dinner
Winter whale watching boat tour, approx. 7 hours
Private transfer hotel to Tromsø Airport on departure

days.title: Arrive Tromsø
days.desc: Fly into Tromsø, gateway to the Arctic and one of the best places on Earth to see the Northern Lights, and take your private transfer to a city-centre hotel. Sitting well inside the Arctic Circle, Tromsø's mid-winter polar night brings only a few hours of soft blue twilight — settle in and rest ahead of the Arctic days to come.

days.title: Tromsø & Arctic Cathedral
days.desc: Explore Tromsø itself: cross the sound to the striking Arctic Cathedral (Ishavskatedralen), its glass-and-concrete facade echoing pack ice, then ride the Fjellheisen cable car up Mount Storsteinen for panoramic views over the city, fjords and surrounding islands.
days.taste: Arctic king crab and Arctic char are Tromsø specialities — sample them at a harbourside restaurant looking out over the sound.
days.experience: An evening Northern Lights Cruise takes you out into the fjords, away from the city's lights, for one of the best chances to see the aurora.

days.title: Snowmobile Safari
days.desc: A full-day snowmobile safari through the snow-covered valleys and mountain plateaus surrounding Tromsø, terrain used for generations by Sami reindeer herders — a thrilling way to cover real Arctic wilderness in a single day.

days.title: Whale Watching
days.desc: Head out by boat into the fjords around Tromsø on a winter whale watching tour — pods of orca and humpback whales gather here each winter to feed on herring, often within sight of snow-capped mountains.
days.experience: Round off the evening with a Night Reindeer Sledding Tour and dinner, gliding across the snow behind reindeer with a taste of Sami tradition.

days.title: Depart Tromsø
days.desc: Free time before your private transfer back to Tromsø Airport for departure, taking with you the memories of an Arctic winter.

about.title: Best season
about.body: Winter (November–March) is the very reason to visit — long dark nights this far inside the Arctic Circle give Tromsø some of the best Northern Lights odds in Norway.

about.title: Weather
about.body: Arctic winter conditions typically -5°C to -15°C, with snow cover from November through April and only a few hours of blue twilight around midday.

about.title: Currency & docs
about.body: The Norwegian Krone (NOK) is the local currency, and Norway is a Schengen Area member so no separate visa is needed for Schengen visa holders travelling from within Europe.

goodToKnow.title: Northern Lights viewing
goodToKnow.body: Aurora sightings depend on clear skies and solar activity — no tour can guarantee a sighting on any single night, though the Northern Lights Cruise gets you away from Tromsø's city lights for the best chance.

goodToKnow.title: What to pack
goodToKnow.body: Full thermal layers, a waterproof outer shell, insulated winter boots and gloves are essential — tours typically provide extra thermal oversuits, but good personal base layers make a real difference.

goodToKnow.title: Weather-dependent tours
goodToKnow.body: Snowmobile safaris and whale watching cruises can be rescheduled at short notice for weather and daylight safety — keep the days either side flexible where possible.

goodToKnow.title: Currency
goodToKnow.body: Norway uses the Krone (NOK), not the Euro — cards are accepted almost everywhere, including on tours, so carrying cash is rarely necessary.

goodToKnow.title: Airport transfers
goodToKnow.body: Private transfers are included on both arrival and departure, so there's no need to arrange separate airport pickup in Tromsø.

terms: All rates are net and per person for the package.
Child rates apply for children aged 2 to 11 years old. One child sharing a room with 2 adults, subject to hotel policy.
All rates are subject to availability at the time of booking. Europe Incoming will endeavour to match the rates quoted.
Rates are not applicable during trade fair periods, major European public holidays, and other major events.
City taxes are not included in the price.
If certain attractions are closed during specific periods, alternative options will be arranged.
All bookings must be confirmed at least 60 working days prior to arrival.
100% pre-payment required by bank transfer or credit card (Visa or Mastercard). All bank transfer charges covered by the Agent.
Vouchers will be issued after receipt of full payment.
